# Experimental Report: Automated Membrane Synthesis via NIPS

## Experiment Details
- **Experiment ID:** 56
- **Synthesis Method:** Automated NIPS (Non-solvent-Induced Phase Separation)
- **Polymer:** Polysulfone at 17.0% concentration
- **Solvent:** PolarClean

## Procedure
The polymer solution was premixed manually without heating (ambient temperature of 25??C). The solution was used immediately after preparation with no rest period. ## Notes
- No nitrogen flow was applied during the side-drop casting phase
- Dry nitrogen flow was applied after blade casting until bath immersion
- No heating was used during the solution preparation process
- The automated robotic system handled the casting and bath immersion processes

# Experimental Report: Automated Membrane Synthesis via NIPS

## Methodology
Polysulfone membranes were synthesized using non-solvent-induced phase separation (NIPS) with an automated robotic system. The polymer solution (10% w/w polysulfone in PolarClean solvent) was prepared at room temperature (25??C) without additional heating. The solution was automatically mixed by the robotic system and allowed to rest for 1 day in a sample vial before processing.

## Procedure
1. The polymer solution was drop-cast onto the substrate coupon
2. Dry nitrogen was applied from the side during the drop-casting process to minimize humidity effects
3. The solution was blade-cast to form a uniform film
4. The cast film was immediately immersed in the NIPS bath (0 minute wait time)
5. No nitrogen flow was applied after blade-casting

## Conditions
- Experiment ID: 27
- Ambient relative humidity: 54%
- Process: Fully automated
- Temperature: Room temperature (25??C)

This experiment represents part of a systematic investigation into controlling membrane morphology through precise manipulation of processing parameters in an automated NIPS system.
